method_atom: an experimental thermodynamic and structural characterization of a dense array of aligned anisotropic rods across a phase transition, in which the system is equilibrated against a controlled external field (a reservoir setting a generalized pressure) to reversibly tune packing density, while multiple structural probes track a symmetry-breaking transition between a high-density phase with long-range bond-orientational order (six-fold azimuthal modulation of a diffraction peak) and a low-density phase with only fluid-like positional order, yielding a direct measurement of free-energy changes throughout each phase and at the transition.
object_atom: condensed liquid-crystalline arrays of DNA molecules under osmotic stress, spanning densities from ~120 to ~600 mg/ml between a hexagonally packed high-density region and a lower-density cholesteric region.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the osmotic-stress-controlled measurement of a bond-orientational ordering transition in packed rods and its free-energy landscape via structural probes; the DNA mesophase and its specific hexatic/cholesteric phases are the domain-specific object.
